stories.title: EU Commission: Blame Berlin and Paris for red tape, not us
stories.summary: STRASBOURG — Stop blaming Brussels bureaucrats for your own red tape. That was the European Commission’s message to national governments Tuesday as it unveiled plans for an overhaul of its rules governing … rule-making.  When EU countries add on extra requirements to the bloc’s rules, it “creates barriers, raises costs and fragments the single market,” said Valdis Dombrovskis, the European commissioner in charge of economic matters. He was presenting the Commission’s

stories.title: Faut-il réformer les commissions d’enquête ?
stories.summary: PARIS — Pour ceux qui voulaient déjà réformer le fonctionnement des commissions d’enquête, le débat autour du bienfondé de la publication du rapport de la commission sur l’audiovisuel public a apporté bien du grain à moudre. Rappel du contexte : les uns pointant le risque que le rapporteur Charles Alloncle (UDR) se victimise dans les médias Bolloré en cas de non-publication, les autres étant trop dérangés par le contenu du rapport pour accepter d’y être associés, les choses se

stories.title: EU Parliament vs. Germany in the battle of the budget
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Parliament voted for an EU budget exceeding €2 trillion for the next seven-year cycle. Germany and its frugal allies will fight hard to keep that from happening.  Negotiations over the EU’s next budget are heating up, with MEPs and national governments squaring up for a fight over money and how to spend it. An overwhelming majority of EU lawmakers on Tuesday voted to increase the overall size of the budget by €200 billion; earmark more funds for specific EU programs;

stories.title: The Guardian view on the politics of central Europe: in search of a new left | Editorial
stories.summary: Social democratic parties are suffering an almost total wipeout, as rightwing nationalism flourishes on the EU’s eastern flank Péter Magyar’s historic defeat of Viktor Orbán in Hungary’s recent election was rightly celebrated in progressive circles and beyond. For the global far right, which has been steadily gaining power and influence for over a decade, this was a significant reverse. But it was no victory for the left. A former member of Mr Orbán’s Fidesz party, Mr Magyar will lead a cen

stories.title: Secrets and ‘lies’: 7 takeaways from marathon Mandelson vetting hearing
stories.summary: LONDON — In Westminster cliché, it was the “Svengali” versus the “Blob.” British MPs held a marathon hearing Tuesday, raking over Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s ill-fated decision to make Peter Mandelson U.S. ambassador in December 2024 — a scandal now consuming his government. Starmer sacked the Foreign Office’s top civil servant, Olly Robbins, this month after it emerged he granted Mandelson top security vetting, despite officials raising red flags. Mandelson was sacked in September over the de

stories.title: Justice department files new criminal charges against ex-FBI director Comey
stories.summary: Subject of charges remains unclear, after earlier case over congressional testimony was dismissed US politics live – latest updates Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The justice department filed new criminal charges against James Comey , the former FBI director, on Tuesday, according to a person familiar with the matter. Comey was charged over a picture he posted on Instagram last year in which sea shells were arranged to say “86 47”, CNN and the Associated Press reported. The po

stories.title: Journalist Andrzej Poczobut freed from prison in Belarus in US-brokered swap deal
stories.summary: Sakharov prize winner was given eight-year sentence after process widely condemned as politically motivated The Polish-Belarusian journalist Andrzej Poczobut, the 2025 Sakharov prize winner, has been freed after five years in a Belarusian penal colony as part of a US-brokered multi-country swap deal. His release has been confirmed by Poland’s prime minister, Donald Tusk, who posted a picture of him on social media, saying : “Andrzej Poczobut is free! Welcome to your Polish home, my friend.” Cont

stories.title: Journalist Andrzej Poczobut freed as part of US-brokered Polish-Belarusian prisoner swap – as it happened
stories.summary: Sakharov prize winner was sentenced to eight years in a penal colony in Belarus in 2021 Polish-Belarusian journalist Andrzej Poczobut, the 2025 Sakharov prize winner, has been freed from Belarusian prison. His release has been confirmed by Poland’s prime minister Donald Tusk, who posted a picture of him on social media saying : “ Andrzej Poczobut is free! Welcome to your Polish home, my friend.” “Both have paid a heavy price for speaking truth to power, becoming symbols of the struggle for freed

stories.title: FCC orders review of ABC licenses after Jimmy Kimmel’s Melania Trump joke
stories.summary: Eight local broadcasting licenses under review after White House launched attack on late-night host over comment Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US’s top media watchdog announced on Tuesday that it is accelerating the review of eight local broadcasting licenses used by ABC , in a move critics see as a clear example of political and regulatory retribution against a disfavored broadcaster. The Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) announcement

stories.title: Hogyan lehet többet kihozni a külföldi cégekből a magyar gazdaság számára?
stories.summary: Többek között a német és európai gazdaságra nehezedő nyomást és a magyar cégek előtt álló lehetőségeket járták körül a G7 Paradigma konferencia első kerekasztal-beszélgetésének résztvevői.

stories.title: A Tiszának nehezíti a helyzetét, hogy ez most Magyarország történelmének egyik legnagyobb összefogása – elemzés Róna Dániellel
stories.summary: Hogyan lehet egyben tartani egy ekkora tábort, és mit mutat a Tisza-kormány névsora? Mi látszódik eddig a Fidesz megújulásából? Élőben elemeztünk Róna Dániellel.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs: Ebben a helyzetben mindannyian bűnhődünk
stories.summary: A Fidesz kampányfőnöke beül a Fidesz frakciójába, de júliustól EP-képviselő lesz, Szekeres Pállal cserél helyet.

stories.title: Tiborcz István a BDPST-n keresztül folytatná az üzleti tevékenységét Magyarországon
stories.summary: Csányi Sándor azt mondta, neki nincs félnivalója, ellentétben azokkal, akik nem megfelelő tenderek révén jutottak munkához.

stories.summary: A szlovák kormány az importtilalomról szóló határozat jogalapját kérdőjelezi meg.

stories.title: Egy színdarabból indult ki, mára országos kampányban hirdetik: az iskolai bántalmazás NEM OKÉ
stories.summary: A magyar tizenévesek 85 százaléka már legalább egyszer megtapasztalta az iskolai bántalmazást. Van, akinek a tusfürdőjét veszik el a társai, mást leköpnek vagy megkergetnek az udvaron. A bántalmazott gyerekek gyakran félnek beszélni, és szégyent éreznek. A Darázs című darab végén felmerül az a jogos kérdés, hogy hol és hogyan lehet megtörni a bántalmazás ördögi körét.

stories.title: „Aki nem képes arra, hogy legyen saját honlapja, az nem vállalkozás, hanem zombicég” – hogyan gyorsítsuk fel a magyar gazdaság növekedését?

stories.title: Krasznahorkai László a szegedi egyetem díszdoktora lesz
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas író májusban Szegedre látogat, ahol egyetemi rendezvényeken vesz részt.

stories.summary: Orbán nem fogadta el a saját lemondását a Fidesz keddi választmányi ülésén, mondta a 21 Kutatóközpont vezetője a Telex élő műsorában. A Fidesz nagy neveinek szerinte derogálna beülni a parlamentbe.

stories.summary: A kárpátaljai magyarok helyzete miatt Beregszászon találkozna az ukrán elnökkel.

stories.summary: Az Emberi Jogok Európai Bírósága szerint ez jogszerűtlen beavatkozás volt a magánélethez való jogukba.

stories.summary: A volt nyomozónak viszket a tenyere, hogy részt vegyen a vagyonvisszaszerzésben, de a Tisza Párt még nem kereste feladatokkal, aminek a folyamatban lévő büntetőeljárás is lehet az oka. Nem akarja, hogy ejtsék az ügyét, csak tisztességes eljárásra vágyik.

stories.title: Magyar Péter az ügynökaktákról egyeztetett, október 22-én hozzák nyilvánosságra az iratokat
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök közölte, vizsgálóbizottságot hoznak létre az 1988 és 2000 közötti privatizáció kapcsán végrehajtott szabad rablás haszonélvezőinek feltárására.

stories.title: A legrégebbi magyar gabona, ami még finom is
stories.summary: A köles gluténmentes, ezerféleképpen felhasználható, és nemcsak azért érdekes, mert jó alternatíva a néha megunt rizs leváltására, hanem mert az egyik legrégebbi alapanyagunk.

stories.title: Orbán may be gone, but his prejudices are now baked into the European political mainstream | Shada Islam
stories.summary: EU leaders have normalised once fringe racist narratives in their migration, border control and even foreign policies For years, Viktor Orbán, with his anti-migrant and white Christian nationalist rhetoric – sentiments that endeared him to Donald Trump and his Maga base – offered his European counterparts the comforting fiction that racism in the EU was the preserve of a few unsavoury men and women. Unfortunately, it isn’t that simple. Racism is not the work of one individual. It is structural.

stories.title: Europe is in a profound state of crisis. Luckily, we know what to do | Nathalie Tocci and Anu Bradford
stories.summary: We assembled a group of the continent’s leading thinkers to assess the threats: their warnings are stark, but the remedy is within reach Caught between Vladimir Putin’s Russia, Donald Trump’s US and Xi Jinping’s China, Europe appears in a state of profound crisis, the narrative about its future often filled with fatalism. stories.title: UK calls on ‘middle powers’ to counter over-concentration of AI control
stories.summary: LONDON — U.K. Technology Secretary Liz Kendall has vowed to work more closely with other “middle powers” to reduce the concentration of power in the AI market — arguing that doing so is in the interest of the United States, too. In a major speech on Tuesday, Kendall said: “AI is now the engine of economic power and hard power,” but warned that control over the technology is becoming “increasingly concentrated.” However, she said that “none of this should be taken as weakening our deep,&nbsp

stories.title: 89-year-old man arrested over Athens double shooting
stories.summary: Multiple people injured when gunman opened fire inside a social security office and later an appeals court An 89-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of shooting and wounding several people in attacks on government buildings in Athens. Hours after the double shooting in the Greek capital, authorities announced a suspect had been detained in the western port city of Patras, reportedly attempting to flee to Italy. His arrest followed a countrywide manhunt. Continue reading...

stories.title: US has 'no closer friends than the British,' Trump tells King Charles III at White House ceremony
stories.summary: Trump’s up-and-down relationship with UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has taken a particularly sour turn over the past several months as the president has sought to rally international support for the Iran war.

stories.title: European airlines will fail if jet fuel costs stay high, Ryanair CEO says
stories.summary: Ryanair CEO Michael O’Leary warns that some of Europe’s airlines will go out of business if jet fuel prices remain elevated. “If pricing stays higher for longer this summer, we think a number of our airline competitors in Europe are going to face real financial difficulties,” O’Leary said on Tuesday, speaking to CNBC at a conference in Oslo. “Pricing has mushroomed since March. stories.title: Sri Lanka police arrest 22 Buddhist monks after 110kg of cannabis found in luggage
stories.summary: Customs officials say group allegedly hid 5kg of ‘kush’ in false walls of bags on return from Bangkok holiday Twenty-two Buddhist monks are in Sri Lankan police custody after customs officials found 110kg of high-grade cannabis concealed in their luggage, the largest ever drug bust at Colombo’s main international airport. The group, mostly junior monks in training from temples across Sri Lanka, were alleged to have “carried about five kilos of the narcotic concealed within false walls in their l

stories.title: Brittany Higgins criticises federal inaction as Victoria moves to protect victim-survivors’ counselling records
stories.summary: Exclusive: State government commits to strengthening laws as Higgins labels Albanese government’s response to women’s safety issues ‘disheartening’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Victorian government will strengthen laws regarding the use of victim-survivors’ confidential communications after a push by advocates including Brittany Higgins, who described her experience of having counselling records subpoenaed as a “violation”. stories.title: UAE quits Opec in win for Trump as oil cartel weakened
stories.summary: US president has accused organisation of ‘ripping off the rest of the world’ by inflating oil prices The United Arab Emirates has quit the Opec oil cartel after 60 years of membership, in a heavy blow to the group and its de facto leader, Saudi Arabia, as global energy markets contend with the biggest supply crisis in history. The shock loss of the UAE, Opec’s third-largest oil producer, is expected to weaken the group, which for decades has worked together to use its collective oil production t

stories.title: Hezbollah drone strikes target Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Ceasefire frays further as Israel also carries out airstrikes and issues new displacement orders for south Lebanon Hezbollah launched several drones at Israeli soldiers in south Lebanon on Tuesday, while Israel issued new displacement orders for south Lebanon and carried out airstrikes, as the fraying ceasefire failed to stop fighting between the two sides. stories.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
stories.summary: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents. stories.summary: MPs rejected a Conservative party proposal for the prime minister to face a parliamentary inquiry into his appointment of Peter Mandelson Q: Was there pressure on you to approve Mandelson’s vetting? stories.title: Starmer sees off major Labour rebellion over call for Mandelson inquiry
stories.summary: No 10 deploys full weight to block parliamentary inquiry bid as MPs warn PM running out of political capital Keir Starmer has seen off a major Labour rebellion over a bid to force a parliamentary investigation into his appointment of Peter Mandelson, but many of his own MPs warned he was running out of political capital. stories.summary: US president says Americans ‘have had no closer friends than the British’ amid recent tensions between both nations Donald Trump has praised the “special relationship” between the US and the UK, as he hosted a ceremonial military welcome for King Charles and Queen Camilla at the White House. stories.title: European parliament urges EU to draw up standardised consent-based definition of rape
stories.summary: Eight EU members continue to include force or violence in their definitions in national criminal codes The European parliament has called on the EU to draw up a standardised consent-based definition of rape, in what legislators described as a crucial step towards addressing the patchwork of laws, some of them insufficient, that now exist across the bloc. On Tuesday, 447 of the parliament’s 720 MEPs voted to approve a report calling for a common definition of rape, centred on “only yes means yes”

stories.title: Afghanistan says Pakistani strikes kill seven and wound 85 in first attack since peace talks

Pakistan denied the accusation of targeting a university. Continue reading...

stories.title: Greek police hunt for alleged 89-year-old shooter on the run
stories.summary: Greek police are looking for a gunman, reportedly aged 89, who opened fire on a social security office and a courthouse in central Athens, wounding several, local media reports say.

stories.title: Germany benefits from work with Ukraine — defense minister
stories.summary: The German defense minister said Ukraine was quick to develop new, battlefield-tested technologies. And the German government announced several measures to increase defense cooperation between the two countries.

stories.title: France doubles down on call for caution over EU-US trade deal
stories.summary: PARIS — France’s EU affairs minister Benjamin Haddad said Tuesday that the EU should not rush to implement last year’s trade deal with the U.S., insisting on the need to add more safeguards. “There is no reason to unilaterally implement a deal if it is not respected by the other side. stories.title: One year after Spain’s blackout, its shift to renewables and grid evolution power on
stories.summary: Though solar was initially incorrectly blamed for crisis, renewables have helped insulate Spain from gas price rises caused by war in Middle East One year ago today, all of Spain, and much of Portugal, suffered through a blackout of unprecedented scale and duration. In mere seconds, a cascading sequence of events burst through the grid and created Europe’s first “system black” event in recent memory. stories.title: Evacuees from flooded remote Indigenous areas in NT housed in compound likened to ‘a prison camp’
stories.summary: Residents must sign in and out at a security gate, and vehicles and bags are routinely searched Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hundreds of evacuees from remote Indigenous communities in the Northern Territory have been housed behind temporary fences and denied visitors after being forced to evacuate their homes in the most vicious wet season on record. stories.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
stories.summary: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the

stories.title: Stranded whale Timmy swims on to barge in German rescue attempt
stories.summary: Rescuers hope to move young male humpback from Baltic to North Sea after being stranded for a month near Lübeck Rescuers trying to save a stranded humpback whale off Germany’s Baltic coast have coaxed the mammal on to a barge in the hope the vessel can take it to safety in deeper waters. Amid intense media attention, the high-stakes rescue mission, funded by two multi-millionaires, is being watched by hundreds of onlookers, many of whom are camped nearby to monitor the spectacle. Continue readin

stories.title: Virginia Giuffre’s brother criticizes King Charles III for not meeting survivors of Epstein abuse
stories.summary: Sky Roberts said survivors ‘still fighting to be heard’ after king, whose brother Andrew was accused of assault by Giuffre, did not meet with them The brother of the late Virginia Giuffre criticized King Charles III for not meeting with survivors of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse during his visit to the United States this week. “Survivors are here sitting with members of Congress, still fighting to be heard, still pushing for real accountability, while many of the powerful figures connected to these sy

stories.title: No 10 dismisses Reeves’s reported plan for freeze on private rents
stories.summary: Downing Street says focus will remain on cutting bills, backing renters and lowering energy prices Downing Street has dismissed the idea of a freeze on private sector rents even as Rachel Reeves left the door open to such a move, after the Guardian revealed the chancellor has been considering it as an option to cut the cost of living. A No 10 spokesperson said on Tuesday that freezing private sector rents was “not the approach we will be taking” after sources told the Guardian it was Reeves’s pr

stories.title: How the UAE’s decision to leave Opec could recast the Middle East
stories.summary: Defection is damaging to Saudi Arabia’s prestige – and could strengthen the US hand in the region The United Arab Emirates’ decision to walk out of Opec is a political as much as business decision, and will reignite the simmering rows between the UAE and Saudi Arabia – which had been covered up by their shared anger with Iran over its attacks on the Gulf states since the start of the US-Israel war on Tehran. In the short term, leaving the oil producing cartel it joined in 1967 gives the UAE the

stories.title: Former Fauci aide charged with concealing Covid records
stories.summary: Ex-NIAID employee David Morens accused of trying to shield correspondence related to outbreak of pandemic An ex-adviser to former top public US health official Anthony Fauci has been indicted by Trump administration prosecutors on accusations that he illicitly concealed federal records during the Covid pandemic. The justice department on Tuesday announced charges against David Morens, 78, of Chester, Maryland, amid a sharply divisive debate over the origins of the coronavirus, which has become p

stories.title: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report
stories.summary: Regime used its isolation after closing borders to escalate killings when global scrutiny disappeared, NGO claims North Korea dramatically increased its use of the death penalty after closing its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, using its isolation to escalate killings when international scrutiny disappeared, according to a report mapping 13 years of executions under the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un . The number of documented cases of executions and death sentences increased by 117% in the

stories.title: Humanoid robots to become baggage handlers in Japan airport experiment
stories.summary: Japan Airlines will introduce the robots for trial run at a Tokyo airport amid country’s surge in inbound tourism and worsening labour shortages Japan’s famously conscientious but overburdened baggage handlers will soon be joined by extra staff at Tokyo’s Haneda airport – although their new colleagues will need to take regular recharging breaks. Japan Airlines will introduce humanoid robots on a trial basis from the beginning of May, with a view to deploying them permanently as a solution to the

stories.title: Iran war: US wary of peace plan postponing nuclear deal
stories.summary: The White House has reportedly not ruled out a proposal from Iran to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts a blockade. However, US officials say any agreement must include a nuclear deal. DW has more.

stories.title: Fact check: Cloud seeding didn't make it rain in Iran
stories.summary: Some social media users have claimed the alleged destruction of a climate research center in the United Arab Emirates caused intense precipitation in Iran. But those claims are misleading.

stories.title: Austrian man admits plot to attack 2024 Taylor Swift concert
stories.summary: An Austrian citizen accused of planning an attack at one of Taylor Swift's concerts in Vienna in 2024 pleaded guilty as his trial opened on Tuesday. The man was also tried on various terrorism charges.

stories.title: South Korea: Ex-first lady Kim handed longer graft sentence
stories.summary: Kim Keon Hee, wife of Yoon Suk Yeol, now faces a four-year jail sentence on charges of stock manipulation and bribery. The court also found she accepted gifts from a contentious church, knowing it expected favors.

stories.title: Japan looks to woo allies with new weapons deals
stories.summary: Tokyo has scrapped a decades-old ban on exporting lethal weapons, aiming to bolster allies as security tensions with China and North Korea intensify.

stories.title: Nigeria's leader battles security, political crises
stories.summary: As politicians gear up for the 2027 elections, millions of Nigerians face a worsening security situation. Analysts say President Tinubu's recent cabinet reshuffle aims to rebuild public trust.

stories.title: UAE quits OPEC in shock move amid energy turmoil
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates is withdrawing from the OPEC and OPEC+ oil cartels as the Iran war drives a historic energy shock and rattles the global economy.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy rebukes Israel for buying grain 'stolen' by Russia
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused Israel of "purchasing stolen goods," namely grain from regions occupied by Russia. Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador on Monday, and now it says another such ship has docked.

stories.title: Iran war: Who in Tehran could shape talks with the US?
stories.summary: It is unclear how negotiations between the US and Iran will proceed, and a breakthrough seems far off. DW looks at who is calling the shots in Tehran.

stories.title: King Charles addresses joint meeting of US Congress
stories.summary: King Charles becomes the second British monarch to address both chambers of the US Congress. Charles and his wife, Queen Camilla, are visiting Washington for an official state visit.

stories.title: Germany's birth rate reached lowest level on record in 2025
stories.summary: As the number of women of childbearing age declines in Germany, birth rates are projected to continue falling. The German Family Association says family-unfriendly structural policies are to blame.

stories.title: What Renault's new EV reveals about the global auto industry
stories.summary: French carmaker Renault no longer sells its brand in the Chinese market, but it's not afraid to design and develop them there. It's just one more sign of big shifts in the global auto industry.

stories.title: Venezuela: German entrepreneurs bet on business boom
stories.summary: Venezuela was once a key destination for German industry. However, after nearly 30 years of socialism, only a handful of German companies remain active there. That could change if the nation opens up to foreign firms.

stories.title: How a controversial gas pipeline deal could further stall Bosnia's EU accession
stories.summary: A US backed gas pipeline could help Bosnia cut reliance on Russian energy. But Brussels warns that a deal with a company linked to Trump allies could put the country's EU hopes at risk.

stories.title: EU Parliament approves common definition of rape
stories.summary: Until now, definitions of what constitutes rape under criminal law had varied significantly across the EU, ranging from "only yes means yes" to "only no means no."

stories.title: What happened when Rebel Wilson gave evidence in court?
stories.summary: The Hollywood star is accused by fellow Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes of making defamatory statements.

stories.title: Iran parades armed women in streets as questions over AI videos grow
stories.summary: Videos emerging on social media from Iran appear to show armed Iranian women parading with IRGC in several cities in an upsurge of nationalist and religious fervour, dubbed as a “jihad of women”, but some clips spark AI editing doubts and remain unverified.

stories.title: Mexican army captures top cartel boss in another blow to Jalisco New Generation
stories.summary: The Mexican army has captured CJNG leader Audias Flores Silva aka "The Gardener," a key "El Mencho" lieutenant, dealing another blow to the powerful cartel amid ongoing violence.

stories.title: Man admits plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
stories.summary: The state prosecutor told the court that police found an almost completed bomb in a search of the man's house.

stories.title: 'I jumped around the house': Sabastian Sawe's parents celebrate marathon record
stories.summary: Emily and Simion Sawe share their pride at the runner's historic sub-two-hour marathon win.

stories.title: Miners’ hunger strike in Turkey enters second week
stories.summary: A hunger strike by workers protesting months of unpaid wages and alleged rights violations entered its second week on April 27 in Ankara, Turkey.

stories.title: BBC reports from scene of fatal Indonesia train crash
stories.summary: At least 15 people have died after a train crashed into the female-only carriage of a commuter train in Bekasi.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.summary: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Will Mexico City's airport be ready for the World Cup?
stories.summary: The BBC takes a closer look at major renovations at Benito Juárez International Airport aimed at boosting capacity ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Louis Arnaud, former French hostage in Iran, describes Evin prison as 'the den of evil'
stories.summary: Speaking to Euronews, Louis Arnaud says the memories of Evin Prison continue to haunt him, as he recalls a place where captives are crammed into windowless cells and where the lights never go out.

stories.title: UAE leaves OPEC, citing national interest in 'a new energy age'
stories.summary: The UAE’s historic decision is a major blow to the alliance of oil producers, just as the global energy crisis is escalating over Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but the Gulf state insists its decision “enhances the UAE’s ability to respond to evolving market needs."

stories.title: Suspected gunman aged 89 held in Greece after five wounded
stories.summary: Five people were left with non-life-threatening injuries, according to Greece's state broadcaster ERT.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran it will not be used as 'political punching bag'
stories.summary: Qatar warned Iran on Tuesday it will not be used as a “political punching bag,” calling for a "comprehensive deal" that would secure the Gulf. In response to Euronews, Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman said "we applaud our partners in Europe for taking the lead" in the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

stories.title: Ex-actor Nathan Chasing Horse jailed for at least 37 years for sexual assault
stories.summary: The 49-year-old exploited his position as a spiritual leader to prey on Indigenous women and girls, the US court heard.

stories.title: Lebanon families face second displacement amid ongoing warnings
stories.summary: Families displaced from southern Lebanon set up tents again in Beirut after renewed Israeli warnings.

stories.title: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.title: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.title: Path cleared for Everest climbers after huge ice block
stories.summary: Experts warn there are still risks of further ice collapses, and there are fears climbers will again have to queue to reach the summit.

stories.title: Thousands filled Amsterdam’s streets and canals to celebrate the king’s birthday
stories.summary: Orange crowds fill the streets of Amsterdam as part of the Dutch monarch Willem-Alexander's birthday celebrations.

stories.title: 'It's bizarre': Californians grapple with revelation that suspected Trump gunman was neighbour
stories.summary: People living near the man charged with the attempted assassination of Trump say they were shocked to see their neighbour on TV.

stories.title: UK PM Starmer avoids parliamentary probe over Mandelson appointment as US envoy
stories.summary: Starmer denies allegations that his office applied pressure on the foreign ministry to approve Mandelson's appointment as Britain's ambassador in Washington despite having failed security vetting.

stories.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted for second time
stories.summary: The indictment stems from a 2025 photo of seashells posted by Comey that critics have said encouraged violence against the US president.

stories.title: Musk says basis of charitable giving at stake in OpenAI lawsuit
stories.summary: The case over OpenAI's history and public commitments could have major implications for the future of AI.

stories.title: US regulator to review Disney broadcast licences after Jimmy Kimmel joke about Melania Trump
stories.summary: The move comes as the White House pressures Disney-owned ABC to fire Kimmel after he called Melania an "expectant widow".

stories.title: Inside Trump press dinner shooting suspect's court appearance
stories.summary: BBC reports from Washington DC, where Cole Tomas Allen was charged with attempting to assassinate Donald Trump.

stories.title: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump greet Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla upon arrival at the South Portico of the White House in Washington, DC.

stories.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors
stories.summary: Efforts continue to free two trapped passengers in wreckage after long-distance train collides with commuter train outside Jakarta, injuring 81 The death toll from a train collision near the Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 14 with another 84 injured, the train operator said on Tuesday, as rescuers worked to extract survivors still trapped in the wreckage. The collision between a commuter train and a long-distance train happened late on Monday in Bekasi, just outside Jakarta. Continue rea

stories.title: ‘A constant quiet terror’: Getting lost in Irish folklore – in pictures
stories.summary: Maria Lax’s images are inspired by the phenomenon of ‘stray sod’, in which patches of enchanted land are said to lead astray anyone who steps on them Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany news: Major raids carried out against Hells Angels
stories.summary: A large-scale police operation in the western state of North Rhine-Westphalia has targeted biker gang crime. Meanwhile, Germany is to bid again for a non-permanent seat in the UN Security Council. DW has more.

stories.title: Canada's Arctic noise is no party for narwhals
stories.summary: Known as the "unicorns of the sea," narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

stories.title: Performer describes locking eyes with Trump as they ducked for cover during shooting
stories.summary: Oz Pearlman - a self-described "mentalist" - was speaking to Trump, First Lady Melania Trump and press secretary Karoline Leavitt as the attack happened.

stories.title: US political violence generates a familiar cycle - this time it's in overdrive
stories.summary: In modern America, it seems violence of this kind has become an ever-present storm that can strike anywhere and at any moment.

stories.title: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington dinner
stories.summary: Investigators say the 31-year-old California man wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible.

stories.title: Rescuers race to free survivors trapped inside train after fatal Indonesia crash
stories.summary: Two trains collided on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital on Monday, killing at least 15 people.

stories.title: King Charles III welcomed at the White House as four-day state visit begins
stories.summary: The state visit to mark the 250th anniversary of US independence comes amid a strain in the UK - US special relationship following Trump's repeated bashing of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer over the US war with Iran.

stories.title: Rare spring snowstorm hits Moscow
stories.summary: A rare spring snowfall blankets Moscow, forcing flight cancellations and toppling trees. The Russian Meteorological Center has declared an orange alert level for the capital, in effect until Tuesday morning.

stories.title: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade, reports claim
stories.summary: Despite an ongoing ceasefire, the US and Iran are locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil and gas trade passes in peacetime.

stories.title: Hetekkel a választások előtt is milliárdos szerződéseket írt alá Balásy Gyula cégeivel Rogán minisztériuma
stories.summary: 22 milliárd forintot szórt ki a Miniszterelnöki Kabinetiroda Balásy Gyula cégeinek az év első három hónapjában.

stories.title: Feljelentést tett a Független Előadó-művészeti Szövetség az NKA által kiszórt, átláthatatlan támogatások miatt
stories.summary: A FESZ szerint a fű alatti pályázatok kiosztása miatt felmerül a jelentős vagyoni hátrányt okozó költségvetési csalás gyanúja.

stories.title: Lelassult a lakáspiac, tovább tart eladni a használt ingatlanokat
stories.summary: Az Ingatlan.com szerint majdnem minden településtípusnál nőtt az ingatlanok eladási ideje, emiatt a vevők most többet tudnak alkudni a vételárból.

stories.title: A Tisza leendő techminisztere már 1999-ben is okosvárosokkal foglalkozott
stories.summary: Tanács Zoltán 2025 szeptemberében, bő 25 év tanácsadói múlt után csatlakozott a Tiszához, ahol a párt programjának összeállítását irányította. Ismerősei akkor rendkívül sokoldalú szakemberként írták le, most miniszterként mutathatja meg, mennyire az.

stories.title: Szaúd-Arábiában üléseznek az Öböl-országok vezetői az iráni háborúról
stories.summary: A Reuters információi szerint a találkozón válaszlépéseket fognak kidolgozni az Öböl-országokat ért iráni drón- és rakétatámadásokra.

stories.title: Egészségtelen összefonódások, torz állami szerepvállalás – a könyves szakmát érintő változásokat sürget az Open Books
stories.summary: Jó könyvek csak szabadságban és függetlenségben születnek, az olvasóvá nevelés pedig a legbiztosabban megtérülő nemzetgazdasági befektetés, írják vitairatukban, ami rávilágít a könyvszakma válságára, de víziót is kínál a döntéshozók felé.

stories.title: Nem változtatott az alapkamaton a monetáris tanács
stories.summary: Legutóbb februárban csökkentették az alapkamatot, márciusban nem változtattak rajta.

stories.title: A készenléti rendőrség nyomoz a vagyonmenekítések ügyében, hűtlen kezelés a gyanú
stories.summary: Azt kérték, aki ilyen bűncselekményt észlel, az a lehető legrövidebb időn belül tájékoztassa a hatóságot.

stories.title: Hiába éltek panasszal a gyanúsítás ellen a mentődolgozók, akik ellen Gálvölgyi János rosszullétének ügyében indult nyomozás
stories.summary: Mindkét panaszt elutasította az ügyészség. Négy, a mentésirányításon dolgozó személyt foglalkozás körében elkövetett gondatlan veszélyeztetéssel gyanúsítanak.

stories.title: „Nem azért kaptam, mert fideszes vagyok” – Deák Bill Gyula reagált a Telexnek a 100 millió forintos NKA-támogatásra
stories.summary: A zenész és a fia szerint minden szabályos volt a pályázatuknál, és látták az erről szóló kiírást. A turnéra fordítható összeget megkapták, és visszautasították a politikai kötődés vádját.

stories.title: Kilép az OPEC-ből az Egyesült Arab Emírségek
stories.summary: Februárban az Öböl menti ország volt a harmadik legnagyobb olajkitermelő a nemzetközi szervezetben.

stories.title: Sebő Ferenc lába nyoma ott van minden tánc után a csűrben, a deszkán
stories.summary: Gyakran elhangzó klisé, hogy örökké velünk marad valaki, de a tegnap meghalt Sebő Ferencre, a hazai hangszeres népzenei és táncházmozgalom egyik elindítójára ez valóban igaz.

stories.title: Befejezték a nyomozást, százmilliós hűtlen kezelés miatt emelhetnek vádat az Integritás Hatóság elnöke ellen
stories.summary: Biro Ferenc Pál azt állítja, semmilyen bűncselekményt nem követett el.

stories.title: Több mint 60 ezer milliárd forinttal megdobná az EU új hétéves költségvetését az EP
stories.summary: A testület kiemelte, hogy az uniós értékek tiszteletben tartása lényeges előfeltétel az EU-pénzekhez, de nem büntethetik egy kormány jogsértései miatt a végső kedvezményezetteket.

stories.title: Hónapokig csend volt a Győr Projektnél indult nyomozásban, mígnem kedden kivonult a rendőrség a céghez
stories.summary: A reggeli házkutatás után délután a városházán is megjelentek a rendőrök, hogy egy másik gyanús ügyben tett feljelentése nyomán kérjenek információkat Pintér Bence győri polgármestertől.

stories.title: Várhatóan kötelező lesz csipet ültetni a kutyákba és macskákba az EU-ban
stories.summary: Többek között az átmeneti időszakok miatt se kell rohanni az állatorvoshoz csipbeültetésre. Alapesetben tilos lenne a kutyák és macskák tartós kikötése szilárd tárgyakhoz.

stories.title: A kormányhivataltól kaphattak fizetést a szombathelyi fideszes jelölt kampánycsapatának tagjai, nyomoz a rendőrség

stories.title: Csütörtök este lesz az első discordos rendezvényünk
stories.summary: Hat órától a közönség segítségével beszélgetünk Hanula Zsolttal álhírekről, kampányról, médiatudatosságról.

stories.summary: A fideszes politikus szerint már megérkezett a támogatás.

stories.summary: Tízéves keretszerződés járt le az év végén, de az ellenzéki városvezetés évekig nem tudott tárgyalni újról a fideszes kormányzattal. Pénz idén is lenne, de valamiért egyelőre mégsincs.

stories.title: Uniós szinten szabályozná a nemi erőszak meghatározását az EP
stories.summary: A jogi következmények nélküli jelentésben az EP-képviselők felszólították a tagállami hatóságokat, hogy a szabályaikat hangolják össze a nemzetközi normákkal.

stories.title: Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz válaszolt Bárdosi Sándornak, aki nem akar a honvédségnél szolgálni kormányváltás után
stories.summary: A leendő honvédelmi miniszter támogatja az egykori birkózó jogviszonyának megszüntetését.

stories.title: 22 buddhista szerzetest fogtak el Srí Lankán, miután több mint egy mázsa füvet találtak náluk
stories.summary: A poggyászukba rejtve próbálták átcsempészni a reptéren a kábítószert.

stories.title: Összekapcsolja a migránsok visszaküldését a kereskedelmi előnyökkel az EU
stories.summary: Az Európai Parlament kedden fogadta el a szabályok megújítását a fejlődő országoknak adható kereskedelmi előnyökről.

stories.title: Ha felvennének a Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatalba, nagyon sok NAV-os aktát újranyitnék – interjú Szabó Bencével
stories.summary: Ultramagas pulzussal követte a róla szóló híreket, és hetekig alig mert kimozdulni a lakásából. A választás napja érzelmi hullámvasút volt, végül az apja azzal hívta fel, hogy nem fog börtönbe kerülni. Szabó Bence a választás utáni első interjújában elmondta, milyen a kapcsolata Pálinkás Szilveszterrel, és azt is elmondta, hogy miért akart színpadra állni a Rendszerbontó Koncerten.

stories.title: Az Európai Bizottság nagytakarítást ígért az uniós jogszabályok között
stories.summary: Az uniós testület nekifut, hogy könnyen érthető uniós jogszabályokat javasoljon.

stories.title: Szakmai együttműködést ajánlott az ELTE-nek az Autisták Országos Szövetsége az oktatói felmondások után
stories.summary: Komoly aggodalommal tölti el a szervezetet, hogy felmondott az ELTE autizmus spektrum pedagógiája szakirányának összes oktatója.

stories.title: Fideszes párttársa szerint Radics Béla árt a Fidesz megújulásának
stories.summary: Az egyéniben kikapott Radics listáról ülne a parlamentbe.

stories.title: Mind a négy lottónyertes jelentkezett az összesen 8 milliárd forintnál is nagyobb nyereményért

stories.title: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
stories.summary: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flames The US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean. This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-Presse. Continue reading...

stories.title: Record bear sightings in Japan cause alarm as hibernation ends
stories.summary: Woman’s body found in Iwate prefecture last week, soon after a police officer was injured in bear attack nearby Rested but famished bears emerging from hibernation in Japan are already coming into contact with humans, with the pace of sightings outstripping that seen in 2025, a record year for bear attacks . According to media reports, the animals have been spotted with surprising frequency in urban areas in the country’s north-east, with authorities urging caution among people planning to spend

stories.title: Mexico warns US involvement in anti-drug operation should not be repeated
stories.summary: Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexico was not aware of US participation until four officials were killed in car crash Claudia Sheinbaum , Mexico’s president, said on Monday that her government told the United States, in a diplomatic note, that the unauthorized presence of US officials at an anti-narcotics operation in the northern state of Chihuahua should not be repeated. The incident came to light after two US officials, along with two Mexican officials, were killed in a car crash on 19 April after th

stories.title: Mali's junta in crisis as rebels seize towns and military bases after killing defence chief
stories.summary: According to a government statement, Gen. Sadio Camara’s residence was targeted by a suicide car bomber and other jihadist attackers on Saturday.

stories.title: At least 21 killed in Russia's strikes on Ukraine on Chernobyl anniversary
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed at least 21 across Ukraine over the weekend and into Monday as Kyiv struck a Russian oil refinery, while Zelenskyy and IAEA warned of renewed nuclear risks at Chernobyl.

stories.title: Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
stories.summary: In a rare interview, Commander Robert Brovdi shared how his unit accounts for a third of all targets destroyed on the battlefield.

stories.title: Italy extradites alleged Chinese hacker to US accused of spying for Beijing during COVID-19 pandemic
stories.summary: The FBI accuses Xu Zewei of hacking US government entities, research institutes and universities involved in vaccine research in 2020 and 2021 at the height of the pandemic.

stories.title: Military action still needed to counter Hezbollah rockets and drones, Israel's PM says
stories.summary: Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war by firing rockets towards Israel to avenge the killing of Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Canada's Carney launches a sovereign wealth fund. What is it?
stories.summary: The fund - which Canadians can invest in directly - will help pay for major infrastructure projects in the country, the prime minister says.

stories.title: HSBC ‘reviewing’ private school perk for bankers in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Hundreds of senior staff in territory benefit from nearly £30,000-a-year grant per child not available to staff in group’s other hubs HSBC is reportedly reviewing a perk that covers school fees for bankers in Hong Kong as part of a big overhaul of the bank under its chief executive, Georges Elhedery. Europe’s largest bank is considering whether to scrap the perk for new employees or make changes to total compensation, Bloomberg News reported . No decisions have been made yet. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
stories.summary: Cocaine-trafficking rebels blamed for worst attack on civilians in decades, which also left 56 people injured The death toll in a Colombian highway bombing blamed on cocaine-trafficking rebels has risen to 21, the government said on Monday, in the country’s worst attack on civilians in decades and just ahead of elections. The attack on Saturday left 56 injured and buses and vans mangled on the Pan-American Highway, in the restive south-western Cauca department. Continue reading...

stories.title: German tourist dies after being bitten at snake show on family holiday in Egypt
stories.summary: Man, 57, was watching snake-charming show when reptile crawled into his trousers, say German police A German tourist has died after a snake crawled into his trousers and bit him as he watched a show in Egypt on a family holiday, police in Germany have said. The 57-year-old man was watching the snake-charming show at a hotel in Hurghada, a popular beach holiday destination on the Red Sea, in early April. Continue reading...

stories.title: SIPRI: Record arms spending, again
stories.summary: Global military spending hit a record high in 2025. The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute, or SIPRI, warns the trend is likely to continue — and carries additional risks.

stories.title: Germany news: Rise in young people refusing military service
stories.summary: Amid talk of a possible return of compulsory military service, more young Germans are applying for conscientious objector status. Meanwhile, Germany has become the world's fourth-largest military spender.

stories.title: Portugal's arms industry is taking off
stories.summary: Portugal has built up a surprisingly large, independent arms industry over the past five years. But whether it can be truly autonomous with the US as a major ally remains to be seen.

stories.title: Will the Iran war end Strait of Hormuz oil supremacy?
stories.summary: The conflict has brutally exposed the energy market's Achilles' heel. While global powers China, India and the EU push renewables, Gulf leaders are advancing plans for new bypass pipelines to safeguard their oil empires.

stories.title: New IRA suspected in car bomb blast outside Northern Ireland police station
stories.summary: Police probe a car bomb outside Dunmurry station near Belfast, with two minors evacuated and the New IRA suspected of the overnight attack.

stories.title: Ukrainians mark 40th anniversary of Chernobyl disaster
stories.summary: Ukrainians in Slavutych on Sunday honoured the men and women whose mission it was to clean up the worst nuclear accident in history. People streamed in from different parts of the city to place candles on a large radiation hazard symbol as they marked the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster

stories.title: Mystery deepens over Mojtaba Khamenei's condition after Iran unveils ‘martyr’ mural
stories.summary: A viral mural showing the Islamic Republic's new ayatollah among dead Iranian leaders has deepened speculation he was killed or gravely wounded in the 28 February strikes on Tehran.

stories.title: Runners battle mud at annual UK charity race
stories.summary: Hundreds of participants waded through mud in the annual Maldon Mud Race in Essex.

stories.title: Trump holds talks with senior security team and makes US red lines 'very, very clear'
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Tehran has blamed the US for the failure of talks after Trump decided against sending a negotiating team to Pakistan at the weekend.

stories.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.title: How Kenya's Sabastian Sawe broke iconic sub-two-hour barrier at the London Marathon
stories.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe on becoming the first person to break the two-hour barrier for the marathon.

stories.title: Swiss voters raise hands in centuries-old open-air election
stories.summary: Thousands gathered in Appenzell Innerrhoden, Switzerland, to vote by raising hands in a centuries-old tradition, electing local leaders and deciding on key issues in a public square.

stories.title: From Castro to Trump: Leaders who survived the most assassination attempts in history
stories.summary: An armed man opened fire near the White House correspondents' dinner, injuring a Secret Service agent and forcing Donald Trump and top officials to be evacuated. Euronews takes a look at world leaders most targeted by assassination plots.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz standoff leaves 20,000 seafarers stranded on cargo ships
stories.summary: Thousands of seafarers are stranded as the Iran war shuts down much of the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global oil and gas trade and raising safety fears.

stories.title: Watch: Seagull imitators compete for the best screech
stories.summary: Participants from across Europe gathered in Belgium to compete in a seagull screeching contest.

stories.title: Russian fighters confirm withdrawal from northern Mali city after separatist attacks
stories.summary: Ethnic Tuareg fighters say they are in control of Kidal following a weekend of nationwide attacks, alongside Islamist groups.

stories.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
stories.summary: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

stories.title: Man charged with attempted assassination of Trump in White House correspondents' dinner shooting
stories.summary: Allen, of Torrance in California, is being represented by lawyers with the federal defender's office and sat beside them in court in a blue jail uniform.

stories.title: Trump demands ABC sack TV comedian Jimmy Kimmel after 'despicable' joke
stories.summary: First Lady Melania Trump lalso ashed out at Kimmel, even though the joke in question had been made in a monologue days before the shooting incident in Washington on Saturday.

stories.title: Germany news: Söder calls for compulsory military service
stories.summary: The Bavarian state premier has criticized the government's voluntary plan. Meanwhile, Chancellor Friedrich Merz condemned the shooting in Washington.

stories.title: Russia, North Korea agree 'long-term' military cooperation
stories.summary: Russia's defense minister and the chairman of the State Duma are both visiting North Korea. One helped inaugurate a memorial to North Korean soldiers killed fighting Ukraine, the other hashed out a new defense deal.

stories.title: Trump evacuated after shots fired near White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Following the incident, US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the alleged suspect who reportedly opened fire near the ballroom where the annual correspondents' dinner was being held.

stories.title: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Security footage posted by President Donald Trump on social media appears to show the shooting suspect running toward White House Correspondents' dinner. Moments later, officials evacuated the area as gunshots were reported outside the ballroom.

stories.title: Who is Cole Allen? What we know about White House press dinner shooting suspect
stories.summary: The man arrested in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner, attended by US President Donald Trump, has been identified by US media as a mechanical engineer from California.

stories.title: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today
stories.summary: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

stories.title: Queer as folk: The gay scandal that shook the German Empire
stories.summary: Germany's last emperor, a gilded porcelain vase and a general who had a heart attack in a pink tutu: The Eulenburg Affair shook the German Empire and Europe to its core and paved the way for the downfall of the monarchy.

stories.title: Sawe smashes 2-hour mark setting record at London Marathon
stories.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history in London as the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He beat the world record by more than a minute.

stories.title: Germany pensions will fall short of living standards: Merz
stories.summary: Germany's state pension will only give "basic coverage" in retirement, Chancellor Friedrich Merz has warned amid hot debate. How does the system compare globally?

stories.title: Moment Trump rushed to safety as gunshots heard at White House Correspondents' Dinner
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump was quickly escorted to safety from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after gunshots were heard at the venue.

stories.title: World leaders react to White House press dinner shooting: no place for violence in democracy
stories.summary: Beyond condemning the attack, many leaders praised the US secret service, police and responders in ensuring the safety of guests. One officer was shot but was protected by a bulletproof vest and is in 'good condition', Trump said.

stories.title: On 40th Chernobyl disaster anniversary, Zelenskyy accuses Russia of committing 'nuclear terrorism'
stories.summary: Ukrainians gathered to honour those who died from the accident, as well as the hundreds of thousands who cleaned up after the disaster, exposing themselves to high levels of radiation.

stories.title: El-Sisi warns Middle East faces ‘attempts to redraw map’, calls for Gulf role in any Iran deal
stories.summary: The Egyptian president warned that the Middle East is entering a “critical phase”, with attempts to redraw its map, and called for state sovereignty to be respected.
